# Mock and Table-Driven Testing Reference

## Full Mock Setup

### 1. Install mockgen

```bash
go install go.uber.org/mock/mockgen@latest
# or
go install github.com/golang/mock/mockgen@latest
```

### 2. Add go:generate directive

```go
// store/store.go — top of file, before package declaration
//go:generate mockgen -destination mock_store.go -package store \
//   github.com/myproject/internal/apiserver/store IStore,UserStore,PostStore
```

### 3. Run generation

```bash
go generate ./internal/apiserver/store/...
go generate ./...  # regenerate all
```

### 4. Generated file header

```go
// Code generated by MockGen. DO NOT EDIT.
// Source: github.com/myproject/internal/apiserver/store (interfaces: IStore,UserStore,PostStore)
package store
```

Never edit `mock_*.go` files.

## gomock Controller Lifecycle

```go
func TestSomething(t *testing.T) {
    ctrl := gomock.NewController(t)
    // No need to call ctrl.Finish() in Go 1.14+ — t.Cleanup handles it

    mock := mock_store.NewMockIStore(ctrl)
    // ... set expectations ...
}
```

## gomock Matchers

```go
// Exact value
mock.EXPECT().Get(gomock.Any(), "user-123").Return(userM, nil)

// Any value for a parameter
mock.EXPECT().Create(gomock.Any(), gomock.Any()).Return(nil)

// Custom matcher
mock.EXPECT().List(gomock.Any(), gomock.AssignableToTypeOf(&where.Options{})).
    Return(int64(2), users, nil)

// Multiple calls
mock.EXPECT().User().Return(mockUserStore).AnyTimes()
mock.EXPECT().User().Return(mockUserStore).Times(3)

// Error path
mock.EXPECT().Get(gomock.Any(), "missing").
    Return(nil, errno.ErrUserNotFound)
```

## Complete Biz Unit Test Example

```go
package user_test

import (
    "context"
    "testing"

    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    "go.uber.org/mock/gomock"

    "github.com/myproject/internal/apiserver/biz/v1/user"
    "github.com/myproject/internal/apiserver/model"
    mock_store "github.com/myproject/internal/apiserver/store"
    apiv1 "github.com/myproject/pkg/api/miniblog/v1"
)

func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
    log.Init(&log.Options{Level: "debug", Format: "console", OutputPaths: []string{"stdout"}})
    os.Exit(m.Run())
}

func TestUserBiz_Get(t *testing.T) {
    t.Parallel()

    tests := []struct {
        name    string
        userID  string
        mockFn  func(*mock_store.MockIStore, *mock_store.MockUserStore)
        wantErr bool
    }{
        {
            name:   "success",
            userID: "user-001",
            mockFn: func(ms *mock_store.MockIStore, mu *mock_store.MockUserStore) {
                ms.EXPECT().User().Return(mu).AnyTimes()
                mu.EXPECT().Get(gomock.Any(), "user-001").
                    Return(&model.UserM{UserID: "user-001", Username: "alice"}, nil)
            },
        },
        {
            name:    "not found",
            userID:  "user-999",
            mockFn: func(ms *mock_store.MockIStore, mu *mock_store.MockUserStore) {
                ms.EXPECT().User().Return(mu).AnyTimes()
                mu.EXPECT().Get(gomock.Any(), "user-999").
                    Return(nil, errno.ErrUserNotFound)
            },
            wantErr: true,
        },
    }

    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Parallel()

            ctrl := gomock.NewController(t)
            mockStore := mock_store.NewMockIStore(ctrl)
            mockUserStore := mock_store.NewMockUserStore(ctrl)
            tc.mockFn(mockStore, mockUserStore)

            b := user.New(mockStore, nil)
            resp, err := b.Get(context.Background(), &apiv1.GetUserRequest{UserID: tc.userID})

            if tc.wantErr {
                require.Error(t, err)
                assert.Nil(t, resp)
            } else {
                require.NoError(t, err)
                assert.Equal(t, tc.userID, resp.User.UserID)
            }
        })
    }
}
```

## t.Cleanup vs defer

```go
// t.Cleanup runs after the test (and subtests) finish, even on failure
func TestWithTempFile(t *testing.T) {
    f, err := os.CreateTemp("", "test-*.txt")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    t.Cleanup(func() { os.Remove(f.Name()) })

    // use f ...
}

// Prefer t.Cleanup over defer for test resources — works correctly with t.Parallel()
```

## Benchmark Patterns

```go
func BenchmarkUserBiz_Login(b *testing.B) {
    // Setup outside timer
    ctrl := gomock.NewController(b)
    mockStore := mock_store.NewMockIStore(ctrl)
    // ... set up mock expectations ...

    req := &apiv1.LoginRequest{Username: "alice", Password: "pass"}
    biz := user.New(mockStore, nil)

    b.ResetTimer()
    b.RunParallel(func(pb *testing.PB) {
        for pb.Next() {
            _, _ = biz.Login(context.Background(), req)
        }
    })
}
```

## Run Commands

```bash
# Unit tests with race detector
go test -race ./...

# Specific package
go test -v ./internal/apiserver/biz/v1/user/...

# Benchmarks (no unit tests)
go test -bench=. -benchmem -run=^$ ./...

# Fuzz for 30 seconds
go test -fuzz=FuzzXxx -fuzztime=30s ./pkg/validation/...

# Coverage
go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./... && go tool cover -html=coverage.out
```
